heading: Certain tying arrangements prohibited; correspondent accounts
label: 1972 Certain tying arrangements prohibited; correspondent accounts

children.label: A
children.text: that the customer shall obtain some additional credit, property, or service from such bank other than a loan, discount, deposit, or trust service;

children.label: B
children.text: that the customer shall obtain some additional credit, property, or service from a bank holding company of such bank, or from any other subsidiary of such bank holding company;

children.label: C
children.text: that the customer provide some additional credit, property, or service to such bank, other than those related to and usually provided in connection with a loan, discount, deposit, or trust service;

children.label: D
children.text: that the customer provide some additional credit, property, or service to a bank holding company of such bank, or to any other subsidiary of such bank holding company; or

children.label: E
children.text: that the customer shall not obtain some other credit, property, or service from a competitor of such bank, a bank holding company of such bank, or any subsidiary of such bank holding company, other than a condition or requirement that such bank shall reasonably impose in a credit transaction to assure the soundness of the credit.

children.label: A
children.text: No bank which maintains a correspondent account in the name of another bank shall make an extension of credit to an executive officer or director of, or to any person who directly or indirectly or acting through or in concert with one or more persons owns, controls, or has the power to vote more than 10 per centum of any class of voting securities of, such other bank or to any related interest of such person unless such extension of credit is made on substantially the same terms, including interest rates and collateral as those prevailing at the time for comparable transactions with other persons and does not involve more than the normal risk of repayment or present other unfavorable features.

children.label: B
children.text: No bank shall open a correspondent account at another bank while such bank has outstanding an extension of credit to an executive officer or director of, or other person who directly or indirectly or acting through or in concert with one or more persons owns, controls, or has the power to vote more than 10 per centum of any class of voting securities of, the bank desiring to open the account or to any related interest of such person, unless such extension of credit was made on substantially the same terms, including interest rates and collateral as those prevailing at the time for comparable transactions with other persons and does not involve more than the normal risk of repayment or present other unfavorable features.

children.label: C
children.text: No bank which maintains a correspondent account at another bank shall make an extension of credit to an executive officer or director of, or to any person who directly or indirectly acting through or in concert with one or more persons owns, controls, or has the power to vote more than 10 per centum of any class of voting securities of, such other bank or to any related interest of such person, unless such extension of credit is made on substantially the same terms, including interest rates and collateral as those prevailing at the time for comparable transactions with other persons and does not involve more than the normal risk of repayment or present other unfavorable features.

children.label: D
children.text: No bank which has outstanding an extension of credit to an executive officer or director of, or to any person who directly or indirectly or acting through or in concert with one or more persons owns, controls, or has the power to vote more than 10 per centum of any class of voting securities of, another bank or to any related interest of such person shall open a correspondent account at such other bank, unless such extension of credit was made on substantially the same terms, including interest rates and collateral as those prevailing at the time for comparable transactions with other persons and does not involve more than the normal risk of repayment or present other unfavorable features.

children.label: E
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “extension of credit” shall have the meaning prescribed by the Board pursuant to , and the term “executive officer” shall have the same meaning given it under .section 375b of this titlesection 375a of this title

children.heading: .—First tier
children.label: i .—First tier
children.text: Any bank which, and any institution-affiliated party (within the meaning of ) with respect to such bank who, violates any provision of this paragraph shall forfeit and pay a civil penalty of not more than $5,000 for each day during which such violation continues.section 1813(u) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: commits any violation described in clause (i);

children.label: bb
children.text: recklessly engages in an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the affairs of such bank; or

children.label: cc
children.text: breaches any fiduciary duty;

children.label: aa
children.text: is part of a pattern of misconduct;

children.label: bb
children.text: causes or is likely to cause more than a minimal loss to such bank; or

children.label: cc
children.text: results in pecuniary gain or other benefit to such party,

children.label: aa
children.text: commits any violation described in clause (i);

children.label: bb
children.text: engages in any unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the affairs of such bank; or

children.label: cc
children.text: breaches any fiduciary duty; and

children.label: II
children.text: knowingly or recklessly causes a substantial loss to such bank or a substantial pecuniary gain or other benefit to such party by reason of such violation, practice, or breach,

children.heading: (iii).—Maximum amounts of penalties for any violation described in clause
children.label: iv (iii).—Maximum amounts of penalties for any violation described in clause
I
children.text: in the case of any person other than a bank, an amount to not exceed $1,000,000; and

children.label: bb
children.text: 1 percent of the total assets of such bank.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a national bank, by the Comptroller of the Currency;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a State member bank, by the Board; and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of an insured nonmember State bank, by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation,

children.heading: .—Hearing
children.label: vi .—Hearing
children.text: The bank or other person against whom any penalty is assessed under this subparagraph shall be afforded an agency hearing if such bank or person submits a request for such hearing within 20 days after the issuance of the notice of assessment. shall apply to any proceeding under this subparagraph.Section 1818(h) of this title

children.heading: .—Disbursement
children.label: vii .—Disbursement
children.text: All penalties collected under authority of this subsection shall be deposited into the Treasury.

children.heading: .—“Violate” defined
children.label: viii .—“Violate” defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “violate” includes any action (alone or with another or others) for or toward causing, bringing about, participating in, counseling, or aiding or abetting a violation.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: ix .—Regulations
children.text: The Comptroller of the Currency, the Board, and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation shall prescribe regulations establishing such procedures as may be necessary to carry out this subparagraph.

children.label: i
children.text: the term “bank” includes a mutual savings bank, a savings bank, and a savings association (as those terms are defined in );section 1813 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the term “related interests of such persons” includes any company controlled by such executive officer, director, or person, or any political or campaign committee the funds or services of which will benefit such executive officer, director, or person or which is controlled by such executive officer, director, or person; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the terms “control of a company” and “company” have the same meaning as under .section 375b of this title

children.heading: .—Notice Under This Section After Separation From Service
children.label: H .—Notice Under This Section After Separation From Service
children.text: The resignation, termination of employment or participation, or separation of an institution-affiliated party (within the meaning of ) with respect to such a bank (including a separation caused by the closing of such a bank) shall not affect the jurisdiction and authority of the appropriate Federal banking agency to issue any notice and proceed under this section against any such party, if such notice is served before the end of the 6-year period beginning on the date such party ceased to be such a party with respect to such bank (whether such date occurs before, on, or after ).section 1813(u) of this titleAugust 9, 1989
